article.body_preview:
By Sumi Khan

Dhaka, Feb 15 (IANS) Rampal power plant, a joint venture of Indian state-owned NTPC and Bangladesh Power Development Board (BPDB), will resume its operations from Wednesday.

Unit-1 of the plant, having 660 MW capacity, was forced to shut down on January 14 due to shortage of coal. The authorities of the power plant were unable to open any letter of credit (LC) to import coal due to the dollar crisis.

After a lot of persuasion at the policy level, LC-opening was allowed for import

llm_connections.evidence_quote: The authorities of the power plant were unable to open any letter of credit (LC) to import coal due to the dollar crisis.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly links a national 'dollar crisis' to inability to open LCs for importing coal, which is a currency-related constraint directly affecting imports as described by the feature.

verification.quote_best_match: The authorities of the power plant were unable to open any letter of credit (LC) to import coal due to the dollar crisis.
verification.judge_reasoning: The article contains the exact line stating the plant "were unable to open any letter of credit (LC) to import coal due to the dollar crisis," which explicitly asserts a cause (dollar crisis) leading to blocked import financing; the issue is described as a broader 'dollar crisis' affecting the plant's ability to open LCs, matching the country-level currency-related scope.
